_comment: Per-chart narratives. CI (build_prompt.py -> GitHub Models -> write_narrative.py) overwrites these from live numbers; this committed copy is the fallback so the page always reads well. source = 'fallback' | 'github-models'.

notes.shape: The yield curve graphs US Treasury yields across maturities from 1 month to 30 years, with an upward slope (longer maturities higher yields) being normal. An inverted curve occurs when short-term yields exceed long-term yields, historically preceding recessions by 6-18 months. The current 10-year Treasury yield is 4.38% with a 2s10s spread of +0.3 points, indicating a mildly upward sloping curve. This contrasts with the deepest inversion in recent history of -0.2 points during 2022-2024 when the curve was downward sloping.
notes.signal: The 2s10s spread is a widely monitored recession indicator, with inversion signaling potential economic downturns. The series' deepest inversion at -0.2 points occurred during 2022-2024; the spread has since re-steepened above zero to 0.3 points, implying a normalization. The NY Fed recession probability stands at 31%, reflecting moderate risk. It is important to note that the yield curve often un-inverts shortly before recessions arrive, making timing signals complex.
notes.now: Currently, the modestly upward sloping curve and positive 2s10s spread suggest a reduction in immediate recession risk compared to the recent inversion period. However, the NY Fed's 31% recession probability indicates that economic caution remains warranted. Overall, the curve signals a more neutral stance without conclusively timing the onset of a downturn.
